what: Restore the tl.multiple_of/tl.max_contiguous quant hints on top of the padded split-K=7 + 4x32 gluon-reduction branch
why: The checked-in v305 regressed both the split path and the fused-quant compiler hints. v280/v295 showed the split-K=7 branch is strongest on BM, while v263’s stride/alignment hints improved the quant path independently.

what: Keep the restored quant hints but switch the repaired split-K=7 branch to the 8x32 gluon reduction tile
why: v280 is the strongest recent benchmarked branch, but it was measured without the later quant-kernel contiguity/alignment hints. This isolates whether the v263 hint win composes better with 8x32 than with 4x32.

what: Re-test the real split-8 branch with the restored quant-kernel contiguity and stride hints
why: The split-8 branch used a different main-kernel geometry (BSK=128, NUM_KSPLIT=8), but the recorded experiments predate the restored v263-style fused-quant hints and do not appear in the current benchmark ledger. This checks whether extra main-kernel parallelism plus the quant hint win beats the repaired split-K=7 path.

what: Keep split-K=7 plus 8x32 with restored quant hints, but lower the large-K waves_per_eu hint to 1
why: The prior waves_per_eu=1 test was on an older split path and before the repaired padded max-ksplit branch plus restored quant hints. If the repaired 8x32 path is limited by register pressure rather than block count, a lower occupancy hint may help.

what: Keep split-K=7 plus 4x32 with restored quant hints, but lower the large-K waves_per_eu hint to 1
why: v306 already passed test, and v309 will cover waves=1 on the 8x32 branch. This checks whether the lower occupancy hint helps the denser 4x32 reduction path instead.

what: Keep split-K=7 plus 8x32 with restored quant hints, but drop the large-K waves_per_eu hint to 0
why: v307 established 8x32 plus restored quant hints as the current BM leader, and v309 is already testing waves=1. The remaining cheap occupancy sweep is to remove the hint entirely and let the compiler choose.

what: Add the older tl.assume extent/inner-stride facts on top of the current v307-style split-K=7 8x32 quant-hints branch
why: The current BM leader restored tl.multiple_of and tl.max_contiguous, but not the positive-extent and unit-stride assumptions that were tried earlier on a different baseline. This is a cheap compiler-only delta on the current winning geometry.
